title: The Tea Party.
content:
In the pleasant green Garden
    We sat down to tea;
    "Do you take sugar?" and
    "Do you take milk?"
    She'd got a new gown on 
    A smart one of silk.
    We all were so happy
    As happy could be,
    On that bright Summer's day
    When she asked us to tea.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Kate Greenaway, titled "The Tea Party.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
